title: 5 Tips To Avoid Losing Your Car Keys
excerpt: Worried about losing your car keys? Be Secure Locksmith offers five tips to avoid losing your car keys. Contact us today to schedule Gainesville locksmith services!
author: Netta Kaiden
metaTitle: How To Avoid Losing Your Car Keys
metaDescription: Worried about losing your car keys? Be Secure Locksmith offers five tips to avoid losing your car keys. Contact us today to schedule Gainesville locksmith services!
body: Many people try to put their things in a safe place where they will remember, only to forget where that safe place is. We all know the scramble to find your car keys in a hurry, only to find them five minutes later. Losing your car keys can be incredibly stressful and inconvenient. Thankfully, our experienced locksmiths in Gainesville, FL, are here to offer you some tips on how to avoid frantically searching for your keys.
If you have to replace your lost car key, the Gainesville locksmiths at Be Secure Locksmith are here to help! Contact us today to schedule locksmith services!
Always Put Them In The Same Spot

Humans are creatures of habit, so you will be less likely to forget to do something or where something is by making it a habit. We recommend developing the routine of placing your keys in the same spot every time you walk into your home. Where you put them isn’t important but make sure it is the same spot, so you’ll always know where to find them. Near the front door is a great spot so you can grab them on your way out.

Another good habit to get into is checking your pockets or purse before leaving your apartment, house, or even your car! Ensure you have your car keys on hand when you are out in public or getting out of your car to avoid getting locked out of your vehicle. Be Secure Locksmith offers emergency lockout services to quickly get you back on the road if you get locked out of your car.
Put Them On A Key Hook Or Tray

Placing your car keys on a hook or in a tray can help reduce the risk of them falling between some cushions or getting snatched up by children. We recommend installing a key hook so that car keys won’t get knocked down and so you know where the keys are at all times. You can also place your keys in a tray on a higher shelf, where they will be out of reach.

Thanks to technological advances, there are ways you can track your keys. These handy gadgets can range from a keychain that beeps to alert you of your keys’ location to a GPS tracker that can pinpoint the exact location of your car keys via a smartphone app. One of the most popular ways to keep tabs on your car keys is to invest in a Tile Bluetooth tracker.

You don’t have to wait until you lose your keys to invest in a car key copy to keep on hand. If you lose your car keys, you’ll still be able to get where you need to go without being stranded in the Florida sun with no access to your car. Like your normal car keys, we recommend keeping these in the same spot at all times.
